result.summary: Plaid exposes a REST API with OpenAPI, a hosted Dashboard MCP server, and a Homebrew-distributed CLI; all require Plaid-issued credentials.
result.description: Plaid provides financial-data connectivity infrastructure that lets applications connect to users’ bank accounts and access products like transactions, balances, identity, transfers, and related fintech workflows. It also provides developer tooling through a dashboard, CLI, and MCP server for diagnostics and analytics.

plaid_client_creds.label: Plaid API client ID and secret
plaid_client_creds.setup: Create a Plaid account in the [Dashboard](https://dashboard.plaid.com/) and open [API Keys](https://dashboard.plaid.com/developers/keys). Copy your `client_id` and the environment-specific `secret` (Sandbox and, after approval, Production).

plaid_oauth_token.label: Plaid OAuth access token
plaid_oauth_token.setup: First obtain your Plaid API `client_id` and `secret` from [API Keys](https://dashboard.plaid.com/developers/keys). Then call [`/oauth/token`](https://plaid.com/docs/api/oauth/#oauthtoken) with grant type `client_credentials` and the needed scope such as `mcp:dashboard` to mint an OAuth `access_token` (and `refresh_token`).

plaid_cli_login.label: Plaid CLI local login/session
plaid_cli_login.setup: Install the CLI from [Plaid CLI docs](https://plaid.com/docs/resources/cli/) with `brew install plaid/plaid-cli/plaid`, then run `plaid login`. This opens the [Dashboard](https://dashboard.plaid.com/) in a browser, stores tokens locally, and lets the CLI fetch and refresh your API keys automatically.

requiredHeaders.name: Content-Type
requiredHeaders.description: All requests must include JSON content type.
